features.name: Widget System
features.description: Composable widget tree with lifecycle management, mounting, and DOM-like querying.

features.name: CSS Styling
features.description: Subset of CSS for terminal UI styling with selectors, variables, and responsive layouts.

features.name: Reactive Properties
features.description: Descriptor-based reactive data binding that triggers re-renders and watchers on value changes.

features.name: Message System
features.description: Event-driven message passing between widgets with bubbling, capturing, and handler discovery.

features.name: Screen Stack
features.description: Modal screen management with push/pop navigation and background screen composition.

features.name: Key Bindings
features.description: Declarative key binding system using Binding objects with action method dispatch.

features.name: Layout Engine
features.description: Flexbox-inspired layout system for arranging widgets in rows, columns, and grid layouts.

features.name: Worker System
features.description: Managed async workers for running background tasks with cancellation and lifecycle callbacks.

call_chain.role: Override point where widgets yield their child widgets to build the widget tree.
Attaches child widgets to the DOM, triggers lifecycle events, and schedules initial renders.
CSS selector-based DOM querying for finding widgets by type, class, or ID.
Returns a Rich renderable representing the widget's visual content for the current frame.
Top-level compose that defines the application's root widget hierarchy.

widget_system.explanation: Textual's widget system follows a DOM-like tree structure. Apps and Widgets define their children by overriding compose(), which yields Widget instances. When the app starts, these are mounted into a tree via mount(), triggering on_mount lifecycle events. Each widget has a render() method that returns Rich renderables for drawing. The DOM supports CSS-style querying via query() for finding widgets by selector. This architecture enables reactive updates — when a widget's state changes, only its subtree is re-rendered, similar to React's virtual DOM but for terminal UIs.

call_chain.role: Descriptor __set__ that stores the new value and triggers watchers and validation.
Discovers and invokes watch_* methods on the owning widget when a reactive value changes.
Descriptor __get__ that retrieves the current value from the widget's internal storage.
Schedules a re-render of the widget, optionally including its children and layout.

reactive_properties.explanation: Textual's reactive system uses Python descriptors. When you define 'count = reactive(0)' on a Widget class, it creates a descriptor that intercepts attribute access. On __set__, the descriptor stores the new value, then calls _run_watchers() which discovers 'watch_count()' methods on the widget (by naming convention). Watchers can update UI state, trigger animations, or modify other reactives. If the reactive is marked as layout=True, changing it also triggers a layout reflow. This provides a React-like reactive data binding pattern entirely through Python's descriptor protocol.
